summary: Recent updates in the Web Audio area have focused on _hardening_ the buffer sizing during audio decoding. This enhancement aims to improve stability and performance when handling various audio formats, ensuring smoother playback experiences and reducing the chances of audio glitches or delays. By optimizing how audio data is buffered, Firefox enhances overall media performance and user satisfaction, especially during intensive tasks such as streaming or gaming. While users may not notice any direct changes _immediately_, these improvements lay the groundwork for a more robust audio experience in future updates.
